Package neqsim.process.equipment.distillation

Class DistillationColumn

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.process.SimulationBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.ProcessEquipmentBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.distillation.DistillationColumn
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, DistillationInterface, ProcessEquipmentInterface, ProcessElementInterface, SimulationInterface, NamedInterface
Direct Known Subclasses:
PackedColumn, ScrubColumn
public class DistillationColumn extends ProcessEquipmentBaseClass implements DistillationInterface
Models a tray based distillation column with optional condenser and reboiler.

The column is solved using a sequential substitution approach. The init() method sets initial tray temperatures by running the feed tray and linearly distributing temperatures towards the top and bottom. During run(UUID) the trays are iteratively solved in upward and downward sweeps until the summed temperature change between iterations is below the configured temperatureTolerance or the iteration limit is reached.

Author:
esol
See Also:

    Create an economic optimization result.
    private DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationMetrics
    createEmptyEconomicTrayOptimizationMetrics()

    Create an unsuccessful shortcut initialization result.

    createHomotopySpecification(ColumnSpecification specification, double startValue, double fraction)

    createMiddleTray(String name, int middleTrayIndex)

    createNormalizedPhaseSystem(SystemInterface sourceSystem, int phaseIndex)
    Create a phase system normalized so all selected phase products sum to total inventory.

    createTrayOptimizationBudgetMessage(int evaluatedCases, long optimizationStartNanos)

    displayResult()
    displayResult.
    void
    enableHydraulicPressureDropCoupling(String internalsType)
    Convenience method for enabling hydraulic pressure-drop coupling with an internals type.

    energyBalanceCheck()
    Prints a simple energy balance for each tray to the console.
    private double
    enforceSideDrawFeedInventoryLimit()
    Limit side-product fractions so side draws cannot remove more component inventory than feeds.
    private void
    ensurePerStageMurphreeEfficiencyArray()
    Ensure that the per-stage Murphree efficiency array exists and matches the current stage count.
    private void
    ensureSeedTemperatureArray()
    Ensure the seed-temperature array exists and matches the current stage count.

    estimateBottomFeedProfileTemperature(double feedTemperature)

    estimateFeedTemperature()

    estimateFeedTrayNumber(double feedTemperature)

    estimateFeedTrayNumber(StreamInterface inputStream)

    estimateTopFeedProfileTemperature(double feedTemperature, double bottomTemperature)

    evaluateSpecError(ColumnSpecification spec)

    evaluateSpecErrorSafely(ColumnSpecification spec)

    evaluateSpecValueSafely(ColumnSpecification spec)
    Evaluate the current value represented by a specification without subtracting its target.

    Finalise a successful solver run by updating iteration metrics and product streams.

    boolean
    isEnforceMeshResidualTolerance()
    Check if convergence currently requires the latest MESH residual vector to satisfy tolerance.
    private boolean
    isFeedTrayNearBottom(int feedTrayNumber)
    Check whether a feed tray is close to the reboiler/bottom of the column.
    private boolean
    isFeedTrayNearTop(int feedTrayNumber)
    Check whether a feed tray is close to the condenser/top of the column.
    private boolean
    isFiniteVector(double[] values)
    Check whether all values in an array are finite.
    boolean
    isHydraulicPressureDropCouplingEnabled()
    Check whether hydraulic pressure-drop coupling is enabled.
    private boolean
    isInReactiveSection(int middleTrayIndex)
    Check whether a middle-tray index falls inside the reactive section.
    private boolean
    isInternalTrayTrafficStreamName(String streamName)
    Check whether a stream name belongs to generated internal column traffic.
    boolean
    isLastColumnTearConverged()
    Check whether the latest outer tear-variable solve converged.
    private boolean
    isLiquidLikeProduct(StreamInterface productStream)
    Check whether a product stream has a liquid-like thermodynamic phase.
    private boolean
    isNearFractionBound(double value)
    Check whether a fraction value is close enough to 0 or 1 to be numerically active.
    private boolean
    isPositiveFinite(double value)
    Check whether a value is finite and positive.
    private boolean
    isPositiveFiniteValue(double value)
    Check whether a value is positive and finite.
    boolean
    isReactive()
    Check whether reactive distillation mode is enabled.
    private boolean
    isResidualGatedSolverType(DistillationColumn.SolverType type)
    Check whether a solver uses a residual-based formulation that should satisfy the MESH residual gate by default.
    private boolean
    isTrayOptimizationSearchBudgetExceeded(int evaluatedCases, long optimizationStartNanos)
    Check whether tray optimization should stop because a configured search budget is exhausted.
    private boolean
    isUnregisteredExternalTrayFeed(StreamInterface stream, List<StreamInterface> knownExternalFeeds)
    Check whether a tray input should be treated as a legacy direct external feed.
    private boolean
    isUsableTemperature(double temperature)
    Check whether a temperature is finite and physically usable for feed placement.
    static boolean
    isVerifyAcceleratedResults()
    Check whether accelerated solver verification is currently enabled.
    private double
    limitInternalFlow(double flow, double maximumFlow)
    Limit a flow to the configured internal traffic cap.

    meshResidualNeedsPolishing()

    meshResidualsSatisfied()

    needsAdjustment(ColumnSpecification spec)

    setVerifyAcceleratedResults(boolean enabled)

    solved()
    Returns whether or not the module has been solved.

    Rerun damped substitution after an accelerator returns a rejected state.

    solveDampedFallbackFromFreshInitialization(UUID id)

    solveDampedSubstitution(UUID id)
    Solve using damped substitution and the configured relaxation factor.

    solveDirectFallbackFromFreshInitialization(UUID id)

    solveDirectSubstitution(UUID id)
    Solve using direct substitution.
    private void
    solveInner(UUID id)
    Run the inner column solver (one full solve with the currently selected solver type) without resetting convergence history.

    solveInsideOut(UUID id)
    Solve the column using an improved inside-out strategy inspired by Boston and Sullivan (1974).

    solveLinearSystem(double[][] matrixA, double[] vectorB)
    Solve a dense linear system Ax = b using Gaussian elimination with partial pivoting.

    solveMatrixInsideOut(UUID id)
    Solve the column with matrix inside-out component balances before rigorous polishing.

    solveMeshResidual(UUID id)
    Solve using inside-out initialization while reporting MESH residual diagnostics.

    solveNaphtaliSandholm(UUID id)
    Solve using Naphtali-Sandholm simultaneous MESH equation linearization.

    solveNewton(UUID id)
    Solve the column using a Newton-Raphson simultaneous temperature correction method.

    solveSelectedSolver(UUID id)

    solveSequential(UUID id, double initialRelaxation)

    solveSingleTray(UUID id)

    solveSumRates(UUID id)
    Solve the column using a sum-rates tearing method.

    solveWegstein(UUID id)

    updateProductStreamFromComponentMolesAsPhase(StreamInterface productStream, double[] componentMoles, String phaseTypeName, UUID id)

    updateProductStreamWithForcedPhase(StreamInterface productStream, double[] componentMoles, String phaseTypeName, UUID id)

    validateColumnPressureProfile(ValidationResult result)

    validateColumnSpecifications(ValidationResult result)

    validateColumnTearVariables(ValidationResult result)

    validateDynamicColumnModel(ValidationResult result)

    validateFeedStream(ValidationResult result, StreamInterface feedStream)

    validateHydraulicPressureDropCoupling(ValidationResult result)

    validateProductFlowSpecificationsAgainstFeed(ValidationResult result)

    validatePumparounds(ValidationResult result)

    validateSideDrawSpecifications(ValidationResult result)

    validateSpecificationActiveBound(ValidationResult result, ColumnSpecification specification)

    validateSpecificationComponent(ValidationResult result, ColumnSpecification specification)

    validateSpecificationHardware(ValidationResult result, ColumnSpecification specification)

    validateSpecifications()

    wasFeedFlashFallbackApplied()

    Check whether matrix inside-out used its matrix warm-start stage in the latest run.

    • enthalpyBalanceTolerance

    • meshResidualTolerance

    • meshProductDrawResidualTolerance

      private double meshProductDrawResidualTolerance

    • maxTrayOptimizationCandidates

      private int maxTrayOptimizationCandidates
      Maximum number of candidate cases allowed in tray optimization searches.

    • maxTrayOptimizationTimeSeconds

      private double maxTrayOptimizationTimeSeconds
      Maximum elapsed time allowed in tray optimization searches in seconds.

    • lastShortcutInitializationResult

      private transient DistillationColumn.ShortcutInitializationResult lastShortcutInitializationResult
      Latest shortcut-initialization result, or null if none has been applied.

    • temperatureToleranceCustomized

      private boolean temperatureToleranceCustomized
      Track whether temperature tolerance has been manually overridden.

    • massBalanceToleranceCustomized

      private boolean massBalanceToleranceCustomized
      Track whether mass balance tolerance has been manually overridden.

    • enthalpyBalanceToleranceCustomized

      private boolean enthalpyBalanceToleranceCustomized
      Track whether enthalpy balance tolerance has been manually overridden.

    • solverType

      private DistillationColumn.SolverType solverType
      Selected solver algorithm. Defaults to direct substitution.

    • lastSolverTypeUsed

      private transient DistillationColumn.SolverType lastSolverTypeUsed
      Solver strategy that actually completed the latest solve.

    • lastSolveStatus

      private transient DistillationColumn.SolveStatus lastSolveStatus
      Strict status of the latest solve.

    • lastSolveStatusReason

      private transient String lastSolveStatusReason
      Optional reason explaining why the latest solve fell back or was rejected.

    • lastAutoSolverSummary

      private transient String lastAutoSolverSummary
      Trace of solver candidates attempted by automatic solver mode.

    • lastAutoFeasibilityReport

      private transient String lastAutoFeasibilityReport
      Feasibility report from the latest automatic solver pre-screen.

    • lastInitializationReport

      private transient String lastInitializationReport
      Initialization report from the latest automatic solver seed attempt.

    • lastAutoSolverHistory

      private transient List<String> lastAutoSolverHistory
      Chronological event log from the latest automatic solver pipeline.

    • relaxationFactor

      private double relaxationFactor
      Relaxation factor used when DistillationColumn.SolverType.DAMPED_SUBSTITUTION is active.

    • minSequentialRelaxation

      private double minSequentialRelaxation
      Minimum relaxation factor used when adaptive damping scales down the sequential step.

    • minInsideOutRelaxation

      private double minInsideOutRelaxation
      Minimum relaxation factor allowed for the inside-out tear streams.

    • maxAdaptiveRelaxation

      private double maxAdaptiveRelaxation
      Maximum relaxation factor allowed by the adaptive controller.

    • relaxationIncreaseFactor

      private double relaxationIncreaseFactor
      Factor used to expand the relaxation factor when residuals shrink.

    • relaxationDecreaseFactor

      private double relaxationDecreaseFactor
      Factor used to shrink the relaxation factor when residuals grow.

    • minTemperatureRelaxation

      private double minTemperatureRelaxation
      Minimum relaxation applied when blending tray temperatures.

    • maxEnergyRelaxationWeight

      private double maxEnergyRelaxationWeight
      Cap applied to energy residual when adjusting relaxation.

    • enforceEnergyBalanceTolerance

      private boolean enforceEnergyBalanceTolerance
      Control whether energy residual must satisfy tolerance before convergence.

    • enforceMeshResidualTolerance

      private boolean enforceMeshResidualTolerance
      Explicit control of whether the MESH residual vector must satisfy tolerance before convergence. When not explicitly set, the gate is active for residual-based solver modes and inactive for substitution and temperature/flow accelerator modes.

    • enforceMeshResidualToleranceCustomized

      private boolean enforceMeshResidualToleranceCustomized
      Track whether MESH residual convergence gating has been explicitly configured.

    • doMultiPhaseCheck

      private boolean doMultiPhaseCheck

    • reactive

      private boolean reactive
      When true, trays in the reactive section use ReactiveTray (simultaneous chemical + phase equilibrium via the Modified RAND method) instead of standard VLE SimpleTray. Set this before the first SimulationInterface.run() call.

    • reactiveStartTray

      private int reactiveStartTray
      First tray index (0-based, inclusive) of the reactive section. A value of -1 means all middle trays (i.e. excluding reboiler/condenser) are reactive.

    • reactiveEndTray

      private int reactiveEndTray
      Last tray index (0-based, inclusive) of the reactive section. A value of -1 means all middle trays are reactive.

    • hasBeenSolvedBefore

      private transient boolean hasBeenSolvedBefore
      Flag tracking whether the column has been solved at least once. Used to seed the sequential solver with the previous tray state on re-runs, preventing divergence from an unrelaxed start.

    • lastTotalFeedFlow

      private transient double lastTotalFeedFlow
      Total feed flow (kg/hr) recorded at the end of the previous solve. Used to detect whether the column needs to re-solve or can reuse the previous result.

    • mechanicalDesign

      private DistillationColumnMechanicalDesign mechanicalDesign
      Mechanical design for the distillation column.

    • topSpecification

      private ColumnSpecification topSpecification
      Column specification for the top (condenser) end.

    • bottomSpecification

      private ColumnSpecification bottomSpecification
      Column specification for the bottom (reboiler) end.

    • specificationHomotopySteps

      private int specificationHomotopySteps
      Number of continuation stages used for adjustable product specifications.

    • lastSpecificationHomotopyStepCount

      private transient int lastSpecificationHomotopyStepCount
      Number of specification continuation stages completed by the latest solve.

    • lastUsedFeedFlashFallback

      private transient boolean lastUsedFeedFlashFallback
      Whether the latest public products came from the guarded overall-feed flash fallback.

    • lastInternalTrafficGuardReached

      private transient boolean lastInternalTrafficGuardReached
      Whether the latest solve reached the internal-traffic guard after divergence recovery.

    • internalTrafficCapActive

      private transient boolean internalTrafficCapActive
      Whether emergency capping of relaxed internal traffic is active for the current solve.

    • lastSolveTimeSeconds

      private transient int lastInsideOutOuterFlashSweeps

    • lastInsideOutInnerLoopIterations

      private transient int lastInsideOutInnerLoopIterations

      Number of simplified inside-out surrogate resets in the latest solve.

      private transient boolean lastMatrixInsideOutWarmStartBypassed
      Whether matrix inside-out bypassed the matrix warm-start on the latest solve.

    • lastMatrixInsideOutIterationCount

      private transient int lastMatrixInsideOutIterationCount
      Matrix warm-start iterations from the latest matrix inside-out solve.

    • lastMatrixInsideOutTemperatureResidual

      private transient double lastMatrixInsideOutTemperatureResidual
      Matrix warm-start average temperature residual from the latest solve.

    • lastMatrixInsideOutSolveTimeSeconds

      private transient double lastMatrixInsideOutSolveTimeSeconds
      Matrix warm-start wall time from the latest solve in seconds.

    • lastNaphtaliAnalyticJacobianColumns

      private transient int lastNaphtaliAnalyticJacobianColumns
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm semi-analytic Jacobian column count.

    • lastNaphtaliFiniteDifferenceJacobianColumns

      private transient int lastNaphtaliFiniteDifferenceJacobianColumns
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm finite-difference Jacobian column count.

    • lastNaphtaliThermoEvaluationCount

      private transient int lastNaphtaliThermoEvaluationCount
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm tray thermodynamic evaluation count.

    • lastNaphtaliThermoCacheHitCount

      private transient int lastNaphtaliThermoCacheHitCount
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm thermodynamic cache hit count.

    • lastNaphtaliJacobianBuildTimeSeconds

      private transient double lastNaphtaliJacobianBuildTimeSeconds
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm Jacobian build wall time in seconds.

    • lastNaphtaliBlockLinearSolveCount

      private transient int lastNaphtaliBlockLinearSolveCount
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm block-tridiagonal linear solve count.

    • lastNaphtaliDenseLinearSolveCount

      private transient int lastNaphtaliDenseLinearSolveCount
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm dense fallback linear solve count.

    • lastNaphtaliLinearSolveTimeSeconds

      private transient double lastNaphtaliLinearSolveTimeSeconds
      Latest Naphtali-Sandholm linear solve wall time in seconds.

    • feedStreams

      private Map<Integer,List<StreamInterface>> feedStreams
      Instead of Map<Integer,StreamInterface>, we store a list of feed streams per tray number. This allows multiple feeds to the same tray.

    • directExternalFeedStreams

      private Map<Integer,List<StreamInterface>> directExternalFeedStreams
      Legacy direct tray feeds captured before internal vapor/liquid traffic is connected.

    • unassignedFeedStreams

      private List<StreamInterface> unassignedFeedStreams

    • sideDrawSpecifications

      private List<DistillationColumn.ColumnSideDrawSpecification> sideDrawSpecifications
      Flow specifications for side-product draws.

    • pumparounds

      private List<DistillationColumn.ColumnPumparound> pumparounds
      Liquid pumparound circuits configured on the column.

    • maxPumparoundIterations

      private int maxPumparoundIterations
      Maximum outer iterations used to converge pumparound return streams.

    • pumparoundTolerance

      private double pumparoundTolerance
      Relative return-flow tolerance for pumparound outer iterations.

    • maxColumnTearIterations

      private int maxColumnTearIterations
      Maximum outer iterations used to converge column tear variables.

    • columnTearTolerance

      private double columnTearTolerance
      Relative tolerance used for side-draw and hydraulic tear variables.

    • hydraulicPressureDropCouplingEnabled

      private boolean hydraulicPressureDropCouplingEnabled
      Whether tray/packing hydraulic pressure drop should update the pressure profile.

    • hydraulicPressureDropInternalsType

      private String hydraulicPressureDropInternalsType
      Internals type used when hydraulic pressure-drop coupling is active.

    • lastHydraulicPressureDropPa

      private double lastHydraulicPressureDropPa
      Latest coupled hydraulic pressure drop in Pa.

    • lastHydraulicPressureDropResidual

      private double lastHydraulicPressureDropResidual
      Latest relative pressure-profile change from hydraulic coupling.

    • lastColumnTearIterationCount

      private int lastColumnTearIterationCount
      Number of outer tear-variable iterations used in the latest run.

    • lastColumnTearResidual

      private double lastColumnTearResidual
      Maximum relative residual from the latest outer tear-variable solve.

    • lastColumnTearConverged

      private boolean lastColumnTearConverged
      Whether the latest outer tear-variable solve satisfied tolerance.

    • lastPumparoundRelativeChange

      private double lastPumparoundRelativeChange
      Latest maximum relative pumparound return-stream change.

    • columnTearVariablesChanged

      private transient boolean columnTearVariablesChanged
      Whether the latest outer tear update changed any manipulated variable.

    • murphreeEfficiency

      private double murphreeEfficiency
      Murphree tray efficiency applied to each equilibrium stage (0..1). Default 1.0 = ideal.

    • perStageMurphreeEfficiency

      private double[] perStageMurphreeEfficiency
      Per-stage Murphree efficiency overrides. Index 0 is the reboiler and the last stage is the condenser if present. A Double.NaN value means that the column-wide Murphree efficiency is used for that stage.

    • convergenceHistory

      private transient List<double[]> convergenceHistory
      Per-iteration convergence history: [iteration][0=tempErr, 1=massErr, 2=energyErr].

    • innerLoopSteps

      private int innerLoopSteps
      Number of simplified inner-loop iterations between rigorous flash updates in the IO solver. Higher values reduce flash count but may reduce accuracy. Default 3.

    • dynamicColumnEnabled

      private boolean dynamicColumnEnabled
      Whether the dynamic tray model is enabled for transient simulation.

    • dynamicColumnModel

      private DistillationColumn.DynamicColumnModel dynamicColumnModel
      Dynamic model formulation currently used by runTransient(double, UUID).

    • trayLiquidHoldup

      private transient double[] trayLiquidHoldup
      Liquid holdup per tray in moles. Indexed by tray number.

    • trayWeirHeight

      private double trayWeirHeight
      Weir height on each tray in metres.

    • trayWeirLength

      private double trayWeirLength
      Weir length (crest length) on each tray in metres.

    • trayEnthalpy

      private transient double[] trayEnthalpy
      Per-tray enthalpy in J. Indexed by tray number. Null until initialized.

    • trayDryPressureDrop

      private double trayDryPressureDrop
      Dry tray pressure drop in Pa per tray — for vapor hydraulic model.

    • dynamicEnergyEnabled

      private boolean dynamicEnergyEnabled
      Whether per-tray energy balance is active (uses PH flash instead of TP).

    • MAX_CONVERGENCE_HISTORY

      private static final int MAX_CONVERGENCE_HISTORY
      Maximum number of entries stored in the convergence history list.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • DistillationColumn

      public DistillationColumn(String name, int numberOfTraysLocal, boolean hasReboiler, boolean hasCondenser)

      Constructor for DistillationColumn.

      Parameters:
      name - Name of distillation column
      numberOfTraysLocal - Number of SimpleTrays to add (excluding reboiler/condenser)
      hasReboiler - Set true to add reboiler
      hasCondenser - Set true to add Condenser

  • Method Details

    • setMultiPhaseCheck

      public void setMultiPhaseCheck(boolean doMultiPhaseCheck)

      Setter for the field doMultiPhaseCheck.

      Parameters:
      doMultiPhaseCheck - a boolean

    • isDoMultiPhaseCheck

      public boolean isDoMultiPhaseCheck()

      Getter for the field doMultiPhaseCheck.

      Returns:
      a boolean

    • addFeedStream

      public void addFeedStream(StreamInterface inputStream, int feedTrayNumber)

      Add a feed stream to the specified tray. (Now allows multiple streams on the same trayNumber, using a list.)

      Parameters:
      inputStream - the feed stream
      feedTrayNumber - the tray number (0-based in the code) to which this feed goes
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the stream is null or the tray index is outside the column tray range

    • addFeedStream

      public void addFeedStream(StreamInterface inputStream)
      Add a feed stream to the column without specifying the tray.

      The feed tray is estimated automatically when the column is run. The estimate uses an existing tray temperature profile when available, otherwise it builds a simple temperature profile from configured condenser/reboiler temperatures and the feed temperature. This is a robust initial placement heuristic, not a guarantee of global optimum or convergence for every specification.

      Parameters:
      inputStream - the feed stream

    • getFeedStreams

      public List<StreamInterface> getFeedStreams(int feedTrayNumber)
      Return the feed streams connected to a given tray.
      Parameters:
      feedTrayNumber - tray index where feeds are connected
      Returns:
      immutable view of feed streams connected to the tray

    • getFeedStreams

      public Map<Integer,List<StreamInterface>> getFeedStreams()
      Return all feed streams connected to the column, keyed by bottom-up tray index.
      Returns:
      immutable view of the feed stream map

    • hasReboiler

      public boolean hasReboiler()
      Check whether this column includes a reboiler stage.
      Returns:
      true if a reboiler is present

    • hasCondenser

      public boolean hasCondenser()
      Check whether this column includes a condenser stage.
      Returns:
      true if a condenser is present

    • estimateFeedTrayNumber

      public int estimateFeedTrayNumber(StreamInterface inputStream)
      Estimate which tray an unassigned feed stream would be placed on.

      This method does not connect the feed stream to the column. It is intended for diagnostics and for checking automatic feed placement before calling SimulationInterface.run().

      Parameters:
      inputStream - feed stream to evaluate
      Returns:
      0-based tray number, or -1 if the stream is null or no trays exist

    • getFeedTrayNumber

      public int getFeedTrayNumber(StreamInterface inputStream)
      Return the tray number for a feed stream currently assigned to the column.

      The lookup first compares stream object identity and then falls back to the stream name. Feed streams added with addFeedStream(StreamInterface) are assigned when the column is run.

      Parameters:
      inputStream - feed stream to locate
      Returns:
      0-based tray number, or -1 if the stream is null or not assigned

    • getFeedTrayNumber

      public int getFeedTrayNumber(String streamName)
      Return the tray number for a feed stream with the given name.
      Parameters:
      streamName - feed stream name to locate
      Returns:
      0-based tray number, or -1 if the name is null or not assigned

    • getFeedTrayNumberByReference

      private int getFeedTrayNumberByReference(StreamInterface inputStream)
      Return the tray number for the exact feed stream object.
      Parameters:
      inputStream - feed stream object to locate
      Returns:
      0-based tray number, or -1 if the stream object is not assigned

    • init

      public void init()
      Prepare the column for calculation by estimating tray temperatures and linking streams between trays.

      The feed tray is solved first to obtain a temperature estimate. This temperature is then used to linearly guess temperatures upwards to the condenser and downwards to the reboiler. Gas and liquid outlet streams are connected to neighbouring trays so that a subsequent call to run(UUID) can iterate to convergence.

    • run

      public void run(UUID id)

      In this method all thermodynamic and unit operations will be calculated in a steady state calculation.

      Solve the column until tray temperatures converge. The method applies sequential substitution with an adaptive relaxation controller. Pressures are set linearly between bottom and top. Each iteration performs an upward sweep where liquid flows downward followed by a downward sweep where vapour flows upward. Tray temperatures and inter-tray stream flow rates are relaxed if the combined temperature, mass and energy residuals grow, providing basic line-search behaviour.

      Specified by:
      run in interface SimulationInterface
      Parameters:
      id - UUID

    • solveWithSpecifications

      private void solveWithSpecifications(UUID id)
      Solve the column with an outer loop that adjusts condenser/reboiler temperatures to satisfy product specifications. Uses a secant method for each specification that requires adjustment.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • getEffectiveSpecificationHomotopySteps

      private int getEffectiveSpecificationHomotopySteps()
      Determine the specification continuation stage count for the current solve.
      Returns:
      explicit user homotopy steps, or automatic robust-mode steps when AUTO is active

    • solveWithSpecificationHomotopy

      private void solveWithSpecificationHomotopy(UUID id, int steps)
      Solve adjustable product specifications through staged continuation targets.

      The first stage starts from the current product value after a warm baseline solve and then ramps linearly to the user-specified final target. This avoids an abrupt jump to a difficult purity, recovery, or product-flow target while leaving the stored public specifications unchanged.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      steps - number of continuation stages to run

    • solveWithSpecificationTargets

      private void solveWithSpecificationTargets(UUID id, ColumnSpecification effectiveTopSpecification, ColumnSpecification effectiveBottomSpecification)
      Solve the column against the provided effective top and bottom specification targets.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      effectiveTopSpecification - effective top specification for this solve
      effectiveBottomSpecification - effective bottom specification for this solve

    • createHomotopySpecification

      private ColumnSpecification createHomotopySpecification(ColumnSpecification specification, double startValue, double fraction)
      Build one staged specification by interpolating from a start value to the final target.
      Parameters:
      specification - final user specification
      startValue - initial product value from the warm baseline solve
      fraction - continuation fraction, where one means the final user target
      Returns:
      staged specification preserving the original tolerance and iteration limit

    • boundSpecificationTarget

      private double boundSpecificationTarget(ColumnSpecification specification, double target)
      Bound a staged target so it remains valid for its specification type.
      Parameters:
      specification - specification defining the valid target range
      target - staged target candidate
      Returns:
      bounded finite target value

      id - calculation identifier

      void solveDampedSubstitution(UUID id)

      id - calculation identifier

    • solveMeshResidual

      void solveMeshResidual(UUID id)
      Solve using inside-out initialization while reporting MESH residual diagnostics.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • solveNaphtaliSandholm

      boolean solveNaphtaliSandholm(UUID id)
      Solve using Naphtali-Sandholm simultaneous MESH equation linearization.

      The rigorous residual solver is warm-started from the current inside-out path. If the Newton refinement does not produce a residual-improving state, the accepted inside-out state is kept. In that rejected-state path, the column products and solve metrics remain the inside-out warm-start values. This preserves the robust legacy behavior while making the new solver an explicit residual-driven option.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      Returns:
      true when the Naphtali-Sandholm solver accepted its direct result

    • finalizeNaphtaliSolve

      private void finalizeNaphtaliSolve(UUID id, int iterations, double temperatureResidual, double massResidual, double energyResidual, long startTime)
      Finalize a direct Naphtali-Sandholm solve without invoking generic product reconciliation.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      iterations - number of solver iterations
      temperatureResidual - final temperature residual
      massResidual - final mass residual
      energyResidual - final energy residual
      startTime - nano time when the solve started

    • storeNaphtaliTelemetry

      private void storeNaphtaliTelemetry(NaphtaliSandholmSolver solver)
      Store telemetry reported by a Naphtali-Sandholm solver instance.
      Parameters:
      solver - solver instance containing latest linearization and thermodynamic metrics

    • finiteMinimum

      private double finiteMinimum(double first, double second)
      Return the smaller of two finite values, or the finite value when only one is finite.
      Parameters:
      first - first candidate value
      second - second candidate value
      Returns:
      finite minimum, or zero if neither value is finite

    • finiteOr

      private double finiteOr(double value, double fallback)
      Return a fallback for non-finite values.
      Parameters:
      value - value to inspect
      fallback - fallback value
      Returns:
      value when finite, otherwise fallback

    • tryGuardedMeshNewtonPolish

      private boolean tryGuardedMeshNewtonPolish(UUID id, double baselineResidualNorm)
      Try one Newton polishing pass on a deep-copied candidate column and accept it only if the MESH residual norm improves.

      Running the aggressive Newton accelerator on a candidate protects the accepted inside-out solution from flash failures, non-finite states, or residual growth. This provides a bounded line-search style guard for the residual-monitored solver without changing the legacy Newton solver contract.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      baselineResidualNorm - accepted residual norm before the polish attempt
      Returns:
      true if the candidate polish was accepted

    • productDrawGateRecovered

      private boolean productDrawGateRecovered(double candidateResidualNorm, double candidateProductDrawResidual, double baselineResidualNorm, double baselineProductDrawResidual)
      Check whether a Newton polish recovered the MESH product-draw convergence gate.
      Parameters:
      candidateResidualNorm - candidate MESH infinity norm
      candidateProductDrawResidual - candidate product-draw residual norm
      baselineResidualNorm - baseline MESH infinity norm
      baselineProductDrawResidual - baseline product-draw residual norm
      Returns:
      true when product-draw residuals satisfy their gate without worsening the overall residual norm

    • meshPolishProductSplitMatches

      private boolean meshPolishProductSplitMatches(DistillationColumn candidate, double baselineGasFlow, double baselineLiquidFlow)
      Check whether a MESH Newton polish preserved the accepted terminal product split.
      Parameters:
      candidate - candidate column after Newton polishing
      baselineGasFlow - gas product flow before polishing in kg/hr
      baselineLiquidFlow - liquid product flow before polishing in kg/hr
      Returns:
      true when both product flows remain within tolerance

      private boolean productFlowWithinMeshPolishTolerance(double candidateFlow, double baselineFlow)

      baselineFlow - baseline flow in kg/hr
      Returns:
      true if the candidate flow is finite and within tolerance

    • acceptSolvedStateCandidate

      private void acceptSolvedStateCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate)
      Copy the solved state from an accepted candidate back to this live column.

      User-facing feed stream maps are intentionally not replaced: they may contain stream object identities supplied by callers and are reused on later runs. The candidate tray network already contains equivalent cloned feed streams for the accepted solved state, and the preserved maps will refresh tray inputs from the caller-owned streams on the next solve.

      Parameters:
      candidate - accepted candidate column

    • acceptAutoSolverCandidate

      void acceptAutoSolverCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate, DistillationColumn.SolverType selectedSolver)
      Accept a candidate produced by the automatic solver selector.
      Parameters:
      candidate - solved or best available candidate state
      selectedSolver - solver strategy used for the candidate

    • setLastAutoSolverSummary

      void setLastAutoSolverSummary(String summary)

      Parameters:
      summary - human-readable candidate trace, or null to clear it

    • setLastInitializationReport

      void setLastInitializationReport(String report)

    • recordAutoSolverEvent

      void recordAutoSolverEvent(String event)

    • acceptDampedFallbackCandidate

      void acceptDampedFallbackCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate, String reason)

      candidate - solved fallback candidate
      reason - reason the accelerator result was rejected

    • acceptNaphtaliWarmStartCandidate

      void acceptNaphtaliWarmStartCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate, String reason)
      Accept a residual-monitored warm-start state after rejecting a Naphtali-Sandholm candidate.

      The accepted state comes from the warm-start solver, but the strategy reported to callers remains DistillationColumn.SolverType.NAPHTALI_SANDHOLM. Naphtali-Sandholm telemetry from the rejected candidate is preserved so diagnostics still show the attempted linearization work.

      Parameters:
      candidate - solved warm-start candidate to keep
      reason - reason the direct Naphtali-Sandholm candidate was rejected

      private boolean meshResidualNeedsPolishing()
      Check whether the latest MESH residual still needs Newton polishing.
      Returns:
      true when no finite residual is available or the norm is above tolerance

      Returns:

      feedTemperature - feed stream temperature in Kelvin

      bottom temperature estimate in Kelvin

      Estimate the top temperature used for initial feed placement.

      feedTemperature - feed stream temperature in Kelvin
      bottomTemperature - bottom temperature estimate in Kelvin
      Returns:
      top temperature estimate in Kelvin

    • isUsableTemperature

      private boolean isUsableTemperature(double temperature)
      Check whether a temperature is finite and physically usable for feed placement.
      Parameters:
      temperature - temperature in Kelvin
      Returns:
      true when the temperature can be used in the feed-placement heuristic

    • findOptimalTrayConfiguration

      public DistillationColumn.TrayOptimizationResult findOptimalTrayConfiguration(double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, int maxTrays)
      Find the minimum tray count and best feed tray that meet a product specification.

      The search evaluates total tray count and feed tray together. It returns the first total tray count that has a converged case meeting the requested purity, then selects the feed tray with the lowest absolute condenser-plus-reboiler duty for that tray count. The selected candidate is applied back to this column and the final solved state is left in the object.

      Parameters:
      productSpec - the target purity (mole fraction) of the key component
      componentName - the name of the key component
      isTopProduct - true if the spec is for the top product, false for the bottom product
      maxTrays - the maximum total tray count to try including reboiler/condenser if present
      Returns:
      structured optimization result with selected tray count, feed tray, duties and residuals

    • findOptimalNumberOfTrays

      public int findOptimalNumberOfTrays(double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, int maxTrays)
      Find the optimal number of trays to meet a product specification.
      Parameters:
      productSpec - the target purity (mole fraction) of the key component
      componentName - the name of the key component
      isTopProduct - true if the spec is for the top product (distillate), false for bottom
      maxTrays - the maximum total tray count to try including reboiler/condenser if present
      Returns:
      the optimal number of trays, or -1 if the spec could not be met

    • findEconomicOptimalTrayConfiguration

      public DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationResult findEconomicOptimalTrayConfiguration(double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, int maxTrays)
      Find the tray count and feed tray that minimize annualized column cost.

      This method searches tray count and feed tray for all converged candidates that meet the product specification, then selects the candidate with the lowest annualized cost. The cost is calculated as annualized installed capital plus reboiler/condenser utility cost using the column mechanical design and cost-estimation correlations. Default assumptions are 15%/year capital charge factor, 8000 operating hours/year, 25 USD/tonne steam, and 0.03 USD/m3 cooling water.

      Parameters:
      productSpec - the target purity (mole fraction) of the key component
      componentName - the name of the key component
      isTopProduct - true if the spec is for the top product, false for the bottom product
      maxTrays - the maximum total tray count to try including reboiler/condenser if present
      Returns:
      economic optimization result with process, mechanical design, and cost metrics

    • findEconomicOptimalTrayConfiguration

      public DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationResult findEconomicOptimalTrayConfiguration(double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, int maxTrays, double capitalChargeFactor, double operatingHoursPerYear, double steamCostPerTonne, double coolingWaterCostPerM3, double trayEfficiency)
      Find the annualized-cost optimum for tray count and feed tray using supplied economics.
      Parameters:
      productSpec - the target purity (mole fraction) of the key component
      componentName - the name of the key component
      isTopProduct - true if the spec is for the top product, false for the bottom product
      maxTrays - the maximum total tray count to try including reboiler/condenser if present
      capitalChargeFactor - annual capital charge factor in 1/year
      operatingHoursPerYear - operating hours per year for utility costing
      steamCostPerTonne - steam cost in USD/tonne for reboiler duty
      coolingWaterCostPerM3 - cooling-water cost in USD/m3 for condenser duty
      trayEfficiency - overall tray efficiency used for actual tray count and column height
      Returns:
      economic optimization result with process, mechanical design, and cost metrics

    • findEconomicOptimalTrayConfiguration

      public DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationResult findEconomicOptimalTrayConfiguration(double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, int maxTrays, double[] condenserRefluxRatios, double[] reboilerRatios, double capitalChargeFactor, double operatingHoursPerYear, double steamCostPerTonne, double coolingWaterCostPerM3, double trayEfficiency)
      Find the annualized-cost optimum for tray count, feed tray, and optional ratio candidates.

      If reflux or reboiler ratio candidate arrays are supplied, each positive finite ratio is tried for every tray-count/feed-tray case. If an array is null or empty, the current column specification is preserved for that end of the column.

      Parameters:
      productSpec - the target purity (mole fraction) of the key component
      componentName - the name of the key component
      isTopProduct - true if the spec is for the top product, false for the bottom product
      maxTrays - the maximum total tray count to try including reboiler/condenser if present
      condenserRefluxRatios - optional condenser reflux-ratio candidates to evaluate
      reboilerRatios - optional reboiler boilup/reflux-ratio candidates to evaluate
      capitalChargeFactor - annual capital charge factor in 1/year
      operatingHoursPerYear - operating hours per year for utility costing
      steamCostPerTonne - steam cost in USD/tonne for reboiler duty
      coolingWaterCostPerM3 - cooling-water cost in USD/m3 for condenser duty
      trayEfficiency - overall tray efficiency used for actual tray count and column height
      Returns:
      economic optimization result with process, mechanical design, and cost metrics

    • initializeFromShortcut

      public DistillationColumn.ShortcutInitializationResult initializeFromShortcut(StreamInterface feedStream, String lightKey, String heavyKey, double lightKeyRecoveryDistillate, double heavyKeyRecoveryBottoms, double refluxRatioMultiplier)
      Initialize the rigorous column from Fenske-Underwood-Gilliland shortcut estimates.

      The method runs ShortcutDistillationColumn, converts its stage and feed-tray estimates into this column's bottom-up tray indexing, rebuilds the tray stack, adds the feed at the shortcut-estimated feed tray, applies condenser reflux/duty and reboiler duty estimates, and stores light-key/heavy-key recovery specifications for later rigorous solving.

      Parameters:
      feedStream - feed stream used for the shortcut calculation and rigorous column
      lightKey - light-key component name
      heavyKey - heavy-key component name
      lightKeyRecoveryDistillate - light-key recovery to top product, 0 to 1
      heavyKeyRecoveryBottoms - heavy-key recovery to bottom product, 0 to 1
      refluxRatioMultiplier - actual reflux divided by minimum reflux, normally greater than 1
      Returns:
      shortcut initialization result with applied rigorous-column settings

    • getLastShortcutInitializationResult

      public DistillationColumn.ShortcutInitializationResult getLastShortcutInitializationResult()
      Get the latest shortcut initialization result.
      Returns:
      latest shortcut initialization result, or null if none has been applied

    • screenSpecificationFeasibility

      public ValidationResult screenSpecificationFeasibility()
      Screen the current column setup before automatic solver candidate probing.

      This method reuses the normal setup validator and adds commercial-style active-bound warnings for specifications that are mathematically valid but likely to make solver continuation or outer tear-variable convergence difficult.

      Returns:
      validation result with errors and active-bound warnings for the automatic solver

      boolean tryAutomaticShortcutInitialization(StringBuilder summary)
      Attempt an automatic shortcut-column seed for the AUTO solver pipeline.
      Parameters:
      summary - automatic solver summary receiving initialization diagnostics
      Returns:
      true if a shortcut seed was successfully applied

      boolean tryThermodynamicProfileInitialization(StringBuilder summary)

      summary - automatic solver summary receiving initialization diagnostics
      Returns:
      true if a tray temperature profile was successfully seeded

    • createTrayOptimizationBudgetMessage

      private String createTrayOptimizationBudgetMessage(int evaluatedCases, long optimizationStartNanos)
      Create a diagnostic message for a budget-limited optimization search.

    • runTrayOptimizationCandidateWithFallback

      private boolean runTrayOptimizationCandidateWithFallback()
      Run the current tray optimization candidate, retrying with damped substitution when the configured solver leaves the candidate unconverged.

      Tray optimization is a configuration search. A candidate should not be discarded solely because the default direct-substitution path stalls from a cold start when the more robust damped substitution solver can solve the same thermodynamic and hydraulic setup. The retry is scoped to the current candidate and restores the caller's configured solver type before returning.

      Returns:
      true if the candidate is solved after the configured solver or damped fallback

    • evaluateTrayOptimizationCandidate

      private DistillationColumn.TrayOptimizationResult evaluateTrayOptimizationCandidate(int totalTrayCount, int feedTray, double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, List<StreamInterface> optimizationFeeds, DistillationColumn.ColumnOptimizationState state)
      Evaluate one tray-count/feed-tray candidate.
      Parameters:
      totalTrayCount - total tray count for the candidate
      feedTray - 0-based feed tray used for all optimization feeds
      productSpec - target product mole fraction
      componentName - component used in the purity specification
      isTopProduct - true for top product, false for bottom product
      optimizationFeeds - feed streams to connect to the candidate
      state - captured column settings to apply during rebuild
      Returns:
      candidate result, feasible only when the column converged and met the purity spec

    • evaluateEconomicTrayOptimizationCandidate

      private DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationResult evaluateEconomicTrayOptimizationCandidate(int totalTrayCount, int feedTray, double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, List<StreamInterface> optimizationFeeds, DistillationColumn.ColumnOptimizationState baseState, double condenserRefluxRatio, double reboilerRatio, double capitalChargeFactor, double operatingHoursPerYear, double steamCostPerTonne, double coolingWaterCostPerM3, double trayEfficiency)
      Evaluate one annualized-cost optimization candidate.
      Parameters:
      totalTrayCount - total tray count for the candidate
      feedTray - 0-based feed tray used for all optimization feeds
      productSpec - target product mole fraction
      componentName - component used in the purity specification
      isTopProduct - true for top product, false for bottom product
      optimizationFeeds - feed streams to connect to the candidate
      baseState - captured column settings to apply during rebuild
      condenserRefluxRatio - condenser reflux-ratio candidate, or Double.NaN
      reboilerRatio - reboiler boilup/reflux-ratio candidate, or Double.NaN
      capitalChargeFactor - annual capital charge factor in 1/year
      operatingHoursPerYear - operating hours per year for utility costing
      steamCostPerTonne - steam cost in USD/tonne for reboiler duty
      coolingWaterCostPerM3 - cooling-water cost in USD/m3 for condenser duty
      trayEfficiency - overall tray efficiency used for actual tray count and column height
      Returns:
      economic candidate result, feasible only when converged and meeting the purity spec

    • applyEconomicTrayOptimizationResult

      private DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationResult applyEconomicTrayOptimizationResult(DistillationColumn.EconomicTrayOptimizationResult selectedResult, List<StreamInterface> optimizationFeeds, DistillationColumn.ColumnOptimizationState state, double productSpec, String componentName, boolean isTopProduct, int evaluatedCases, int convergedCases, double capitalChargeFactor, double operatingHoursPerYear, double steamCostPerTonne, double coolingWaterCostPerM3, double trayEfficiency, String message)
      Apply selected annualized-cost result to the live column and return final diagnostics.
      Parameters:
      selectedResult - selected economic candidate from the search
      optimizationFeeds - feed streams to connect to the selected tray
      state - captured column settings to apply during rebuild
      productSpec - target product mole fraction
      componentName - component used in the purity specification
      isTopProduct - true for top product, false for bottom product
      evaluatedCases - number of evaluated candidate cases
      convergedCases - number of converged candidate cases
      capitalChargeFactor - annual capital charge factor in 1/year
      operatingHoursPerYear - operating hours per year for utility costing
      steamCostPerTonne - steam cost in USD/tonne for reboiler duty
      coolingWaterCostPerM3 - cooling-water cost in USD/m3 for condenser duty
      trayEfficiency - overall tray efficiency used for actual tray count and column height
      message - diagnostic message to store in the returned result
      Returns:
      final economic optimization result from the applied selected candidate

    • computeToleranceComplexityMultiplier

      private double computeToleranceComplexityMultiplier()
      Estimate a scaling factor that reflects the degree of distillation complexity.

      The factor increases with the number of theoretical stages and independent feed streams and is bounded to avoid overly loose convergence criteria.

      Returns:
      scaling multiplier for recommended tolerances

      private int getEffectiveStageCount()
      Count the number of simple trays, excluding optional reboiler and condenser sections.
      Returns:
      effective number of equilibrium stages

      private int getTotalFeedCount()
      Count connected feed streams across all trays.
      Returns:
      total number of feeds currently attached to the column

    • shouldEvaluateBalances

      private boolean shouldEvaluateBalances(int iteration, int iterationLimit, boolean polishing, double tempResidual, double baseTempTolerance, int balanceCheckStride)
      Decide whether mass and energy balance residuals should be recomputed this iteration.
      Parameters:
      iteration - current iteration index (1-based)
      iterationLimit - current iteration ceiling
      polishing - whether the solver is in the polish stage
      tempResidual - average temperature residual this iteration
      baseTempTolerance - nominal temperature tolerance
      balanceCheckStride - cadence for periodic balance checks
      Returns:
      true if balances should be evaluated

    • prepareColumnForSolve

      private int prepareColumnForSolve()
      Prepare the column for a solving sequence by updating pressures and cloning feed systems.
      Returns:
      index of the lowest feed tray in the column

    • solveInsideOut

      void solveInsideOut(UUID id)
      Solve the column using an improved inside-out strategy inspired by Boston and Sullivan (1974).

      Key improvements over basic sequential substitution:

      • K-value caching: previous iteration K-values are stored to track composition convergence and detect stagnation early.
      • Composition-based convergence: monitors maximum relative K-value change alongside temperature and balance residuals.
      • Stripping factor correction: applies a bulk flow correction between outer iterations based on the ratio of computed-to-assumed vapor/liquid split on each tray.
      • Accelerated relaxation ramp: increases relaxation faster (1.3× vs 1.2×) when residuals decrease, enabling the IO method to reach full step sooner.
      • Lazy balance evaluation: mass/energy balances are only recomputed when temperatures are close to tolerance, reducing expensive per-tray flow rate queries.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • shouldSwitchInsideOutToNewton

      private boolean shouldSwitchInsideOutToNewton(int iteration, double temperatureResidual, double temperatureTolerance, double kValueResidual)
      Decide when the inside-out fixed-point stage should hand off to simultaneous Newton.

      Large columns spend most of their wall time in repeated tray flashes once the K-value profile is already well shaped. The hybrid handoff follows the common inside-out/simultaneous-correction pattern: use cheap fixed-point sweeps for initialization, then solve the coupled temperature correction when additional sweeps mostly polish the same profile.

      Parameters:
      iteration - current inside-out outer iteration
      temperatureResidual - current average temperature residual
      temperatureTolerance - active temperature tolerance
      kValueResidual - current K-value residual
      Returns:
      true when a Newton handoff is expected to be cheaper than more sweeps

    • storeInsideOutTelemetry

      private void storeInsideOutTelemetry(int outerFlashSweeps, int innerLoopIterations, double kValueResidual, double surrogateResidual)
      Store telemetry from the inside-out initializer before finalizing or handing off.
      Parameters:
      outerFlashSweeps - rigorous outside-loop sweeps
      innerLoopIterations - surrogate inner-loop iterations
      kValueResidual - latest K-value residual
      surrogateResidual - latest surrogate-model residual

    • solveMatrixInsideOut

      void solveMatrixInsideOut(UUID id)
      Solve the column with matrix inside-out component balances before rigorous polishing.

      The matrix stage solves component material-balance tridiagonal systems using cached K-values and cached K-temperature derivatives. It is accepted only as a warm start; the final products and convergence metrics still come from the rigorous inside-out solver.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • shouldBypassMatrixInsideOutWarmStart

      private boolean shouldBypassMatrixInsideOutWarmStart()
      Decide whether the matrix warm-start stage should be skipped for the current column size.

      The matrix component-balance stage has fixed setup cost and is only expected to pay off on larger columns. Small benchmark columns are faster with the rigorous inside-out path directly.

      Returns:
      true when the adaptive matrix solver should use rigorous inside-out directly

    • computeKvalueResidual

      private double computeKvalueResidual(double[][] previousKvalues)
      Compute the maximum relative K-value change compared to the previous iteration.

      K-values are computed as the ratio of vapor to liquid mole fractions for each component on each tray. This provides a composition-based convergence metric that complements the temperature-based metric, similar to what commercial inside-out implementations track.

      Parameters:
      previousKvalues - K-values from the previous iteration (null if first iteration)
      Returns:
      maximum relative K-value change; Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY if no previous data

    • cacheCurrentKvalues

      private double[][] cacheCurrentKvalues()
      Cache K-values (y/x) for all components on all trays for use in the next iteration comparison.
      Returns:
      2D array [tray][component] of K-values

    • innerLoopIteration

      private double innerLoopIteration(DistillationColumn.SimplifiedKvalueModel model, double relaxation)
      Perform a simplified inner-loop iteration using the K-value model instead of rigorous flash.

      This method updates tray compositions using the simplified K-value correlation and adjusts temperatures via a bubble-point calculation (sum of K*x = 1 condition). No PH-flash is called, making each inner iteration much cheaper than a rigorous outer iteration.

      Parameters:
      model - the fitted simplified K-value model
      relaxation - current relaxation factor
      Returns:
      average absolute temperature change across all trays

    • runBroyden

      public void runBroyden(UUID id)
      Solve the column using a simple Broyden mixing of tray temperatures.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • solveWegstein

      void solveWegstein(UUID id)
      Solve the column using Wegstein acceleration of successive substitution.

      Wegstein's method uses two consecutive fixed-point iterates to extrapolate a better estimate. For temperatures on each tray the acceleration factor q is computed from the slope of the fixed-point map: q = s / (s - 1) where s = (x_{k} - x_{k-1}) / (g(x_{k}) - g(x_{k-1})). The factor is bounded to [-5, 0] to prevent divergence.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • synchronizeTrayStreamsAfterAcceleratedTemperatureUpdate

      private void synchronizeTrayStreamsAfterAcceleratedTemperatureUpdate(UUID id, int firstFeedTrayNumber)
      Recompute tray outlet streams after an accelerated temperature update.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      firstFeedTrayNumber - index of the lowest feed tray

    • solveDampedFallbackFromFreshInitialization

      private void solveDampedFallbackFromFreshInitialization(UUID id)
      Rerun damped substitution from a fresh tray initialization after an accelerator fails.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • solveDirectFallbackFromFreshInitialization

      private void solveDirectFallbackFromFreshInitialization(UUID id)

      id - calculation identifier

      private boolean useGuardedWegsteinFallback()
      Decide whether Wegstein acceleration should be routed to a guarded base solver.
      Returns:
      true while the Wegstein accelerator is guarded by damped substitution

    • useGuardedSumRatesFallback

      private boolean useGuardedSumRatesFallback()
      Decide whether sum-rates acceleration should be routed to the guarded damped solver.
      Returns:
      true when the sum-rates accelerator should be guarded by damped substitution

    • useGuardedNewtonFallback

      private boolean useGuardedNewtonFallback()
      Decide whether temperature-Newton acceleration should be routed to the guarded damped solver.
      Returns:
      true while the Newton accelerator is guarded by damped substitution

    • solveSumRates

      void solveSumRates(UUID id)
      Solve the column using a sum-rates tearing method.

      The sum-rates method adjusts tray liquid flow rates based on the ratio of computed to assumed total flow leaving each tray. This is effective for absorber and stripper columns where the temperature profile is relatively flat. The method alternates between: (1) bubble-point temperature calculations on each tray, and (2) flow rate corrections using the sum-rates formula of Burningham and Otto (1967).

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • solveNewton

      void solveNewton(UUID id)
      Solve the column using a Newton-Raphson simultaneous temperature correction method.

      This is inspired by the Naphtali-Sandholm (1971) approach of solving MESH equations simultaneously, adapted to NeqSim's tray-by-tray flash infrastructure. The method treats the N tray temperatures as the independent variables. A residual vector is formed by running full tray sweeps and measuring the temperature discrepancy each tray exhibits after equilibrium. The Jacobian is computed by finite-difference perturbation of each tray temperature.

      Key features:

      • Simultaneous correction: all tray temperatures are updated together using a dense N×N Jacobian solved by Gaussian elimination with partial pivoting.
      • Line search: the full Newton step is scaled back if it increases residuals.
      • Warm-up: a few direct-substitution iterations are performed first to get close to the solution basin where Newton convergence is quadratic.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • performFullTraySweep

      private void performFullTraySweep(UUID id, int firstFeedTrayNumber, StreamInterface[] previousGasStreams, StreamInterface[] previousLiquidStreams, double relaxation)
      Perform a full upward+downward tray sweep, running PH-flash on each tray.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      firstFeedTrayNumber - index of the lowest feed tray
      previousGasStreams - cached gas streams from previous iteration (updated in-place)
      previousLiquidStreams - cached liquid streams from previous iteration (updated in-place)
      relaxation - relaxation factor for stream blending

    • computeTemperatureResidual

      private double computeTemperatureResidual()
      Compute the average absolute temperature residual across all trays. The residual is the difference between the tray's stored temperature and its thermo system temperature after a flash.
      Returns:
      average absolute temperature change per tray (K)

      Returns:

    • solveLinearSystem

      private double[] solveLinearSystem(double[][] matrixA, double[] vectorB)
      Solve a dense linear system Ax = b using Gaussian elimination with partial pivoting.
      Parameters:
      matrixA - coefficient matrix (will be modified in-place)
      vectorB - right-hand side vector (will be modified in-place)
      Returns:
      solution vector x, or null if the matrix is singular

    • createMiddleTray

      private SimpleTray createMiddleTray(String name, int middleTrayIndex)
      Create a middle tray (between reboiler and condenser). Sets the reactive flash flag when the column is in reactive mode and the tray index falls inside the reactive section.
      Parameters:
      name - the tray name
      middleTrayIndex - 0-based index among the middle trays (excluding reboiler/condenser)
      Returns:
      a new SimpleTray with reactive flash configured

    • isInReactiveSection

      private boolean isInReactiveSection(int middleTrayIndex)
      Check whether a middle-tray index falls inside the reactive section.
      Parameters:
      middleTrayIndex - 0-based index among middle trays
      Returns:
      true when the tray should use reactive flash

    • setReactive

      public void setReactive(boolean reactive)
      Enable or disable reactive distillation for all middle trays. When enabled, middle trays use ReactiveTray (simultaneous chemical + phase equilibrium via the Modified RAND method). Can be called after construction; existing trays will be replaced.
      Parameters:
      reactive - true to enable reactive distillation

    • setReactive

      public void setReactive(boolean reactive, int startTray, int endTray)
      Enable reactive distillation on a specific section of middle trays. Tray indices are 0-based among the middle trays (excluding reboiler/condenser). For example, in a column with reboiler + 10 middle trays + condenser, setReactive(true, 3, 7) makes trays 4–8 (1-based) of the middle section reactive.
      Parameters:
      reactive - true to enable reactive distillation
      startTray - first reactive middle-tray index (0-based, inclusive)
      endTray - last reactive middle-tray index (0-based, inclusive)

    • replaceMiddleTrays

      private void replaceMiddleTrays()
      Update the reactive flash flag on middle trays to match the current reactive mode configuration. Called automatically by setReactive(boolean).

    • isReactive

      public boolean isReactive()
      Check whether reactive distillation mode is enabled.
      Returns:
      true when the column has reactive trays

    • setSolverType

      public void setSolverType(DistillationColumn.SolverType solverType)
      Select the algorithm used when solving the column.
      Parameters:
      solverType - choice of solver, or null to restore direct substitution

    • setVerifyAcceleratedResults

      public static void setVerifyAcceleratedResults(boolean enabled)
      Enable or disable the post-success damped-substitution verification run for accelerated solvers (Wegstein, Sum-Rates, Naphtali-Sandholm, MESH residual). Off by default. When enabled every successful accelerated solve is double-checked against a fresh damped-substitution solve on a column clone; if product flows differ by more than 2 % the damped result is accepted. The verification roughly doubles wallclock time, so it is recommended only for regression auditing.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to verify accelerated results against damped substitution

    • isVerifyAcceleratedResults

      public static boolean isVerifyAcceleratedResults()
      Check whether accelerated solver verification is currently enabled.
      Returns:
      true when accelerated solves are verified against damped substitution

    • setSpecificationHomotopySteps

      public void setSpecificationHomotopySteps(int steps)
      Set the number of continuation stages used for adjustable product specifications.

      A value of one preserves the legacy direct outer-loop solve. Values above one ramp purity, recovery, and product-flow targets from the current product value to the final target over the requested number of stages.

      Parameters:
      steps - number of homotopy stages, must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if steps is less than one

    • getSpecificationHomotopySteps

      public int getSpecificationHomotopySteps()
      Get the configured number of specification continuation stages.
      Returns:
      configured homotopy stage count

      latest completed homotopy stage count, or zero when homotopy was not used

    • getConvergenceDiagnostics

      public String getConvergenceDiagnostics()
      Build a human-readable convergence diagnostic report for the latest column solve.

      The report is intended for notebooks, agents, and troubleshooting scripts that need to know which convergence gate failed and which common modelling choices should be checked first. It does not change the column state.

      Returns:
      multi-line diagnostic report with residuals, feed-tray placement, and recommendations

      private int appendConvergenceRecommendations(StringBuilder diagnostics, boolean solved)

      Parameters:
      diagnostics - report builder receiving recommendation lines
      solved - whether the column currently satisfies its convergence gates
      Returns:
      number of recommendation lines appended

      public void setEnforceMeshResidualTolerance(boolean enforce)

      enforce - true to require MESH residuals to satisfy the configured tolerance

    • getFsFactor

      public double getFsFactor()
      Calculates the Fs factor for the distillation column. The Fs factor is a measure of the gas flow rate through the column relative to the cross-sectional area and the density of the gas.
      Returns:
      the Fs factor as a double value

    • calcColumnInternals

      public ColumnInternalsDesigner calcColumnInternals(String internalsType)
      Create and run a column internals designer for this column.

      Evaluates hydraulics on every tray (flooding, weeping, entrainment, downcomer backup, pressure drop, efficiency) and sizes the column diameter from the controlling tray.

      Parameters:
      internalsType - tray type ("sieve", "valve", "bubble-cap") or "packed"
      Returns:
      a fully evaluated ColumnInternalsDesigner with per-tray results

    • calcColumnInternals

      public ColumnInternalsDesigner calcColumnInternals()
      Create and run a column internals designer for this column with default sieve trays.
      Returns:
      a fully evaluated ColumnInternalsDesigner with per-tray results

    • setSideDrawFraction

      public void setSideDrawFraction(int trayNumber, DistillationColumn.SideDrawPhase phase, double fraction)
      Set a gas or liquid side-draw fraction on a tray.

      Side draws are implemented on the tray outlet itself, so all column solver paths use the residual gas/liquid traffic for inter-tray flow and expose the withdrawn stream separately.

      Parameters:
      trayNumber - bottom-up tray index
      phase - phase to withdraw
      fraction - fraction of the selected phase outlet to withdraw, from zero to one

    • setGasSideDrawFraction

      public void setGasSideDrawFraction(int trayNumber, double fraction)
      Set a vapor side-draw fraction on a tray.
      Parameters:
      trayNumber - bottom-up tray index
      fraction - fraction of vapor outlet to withdraw, from zero to one

    • setLiquidSideDrawFraction

      public void setLiquidSideDrawFraction(int trayNumber, double fraction)
      Set a liquid side-draw fraction on a tray.
      Parameters:
      trayNumber - bottom-up tray index
      fraction - fraction of liquid outlet to withdraw, from zero to one

    • getSideDrawStream

      public StreamInterface getSideDrawStream(int trayNumber, DistillationColumn.SideDrawPhase phase)
      Get a side-draw stream from a tray.
      Parameters:
      trayNumber - bottom-up tray index
      phase - phase to retrieve
      Returns:
      side-draw stream, or a zero-flow stream when no side draw is configured

    • getSideDrawStreams

      public List<StreamInterface> getSideDrawStreams()

      unmodifiable list of side-draw streams currently withdrawn from trays

    • addSideDrawFlowSpecification

      public DistillationColumn.ColumnSideDrawSpecification addSideDrawFlowSpecification(int trayNumber, DistillationColumn.SideDrawPhase phase, double flowRate, String unit)
      Add a side-draw flow specification solved as a column tear variable.

      The solver adjusts the side-draw fraction on the requested tray until the side-product stream flow matches the target. This turns side draws into formal product specifications while preserving the existing tray split implementation.

      Parameters:
      trayNumber - bottom-up tray index
      phase - side-draw phase
      flowRate - target flow rate
      unit - flow-rate unit
      Returns:
      configured side-draw specification
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tray number, phase, flow rate, or unit is invalid

    • getSideDrawSpecifications

      public List<DistillationColumn.ColumnSideDrawSpecification> getSideDrawSpecifications()
      Get configured side-draw flow specifications.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of side-draw specifications

    • setMaxColumnTearIterations

      public void setMaxColumnTearIterations(int maxIterations)
      Set the maximum number of outer tear iterations for side draws and hydraulics.
      Parameters:
      maxIterations - maximum number of tear iterations, minimum one

    • setColumnTearTolerance

      public void setColumnTearTolerance(double tolerance)
      Set the relative tolerance for side-draw and hydraulic tear variables.
      Parameters:
      tolerance - relative tolerance, must be finite and positive

      public DistillationColumn.DynamicColumnModel getDynamicColumnModel()
      Get the dynamic column model formulation.

      public boolean isDynamicColumnModelExperimental()
      Check whether the active dynamic column model is experimental.

    • addLiquidPumparound

      public DistillationColumn.ColumnPumparound addLiquidPumparound(String name, int drawTrayNumber, int returnTrayNumber, double drawFraction, double temperatureDrop)
      Add a liquid pumparound circuit.

      The draw is treated as an internal liquid withdrawal, not as a side-product stream. The return stream is updated between column solves and added to the configured return tray as an internal recycle, so external mass-balance reporting continues to use only true feeds and products.

      Parameters:
      name - pumparound name
      drawTrayNumber - bottom-up tray index where liquid is withdrawn
      returnTrayNumber - bottom-up tray index where liquid is returned
      drawFraction - fraction of tray liquid traffic withdrawn
      temperatureDrop - temperature drop from draw to return in Kelvin
      Returns:
      configured pumparound definition
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if tray numbers, draw fraction, or temperature drop are invalid

    • getPumparounds

      public List<DistillationColumn.ColumnPumparound> getPumparounds()
      Get configured liquid pumparound circuits.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of configured pumparounds

    • getExternalFeedStreams

      List<StreamInterface> getExternalFeedStreams(int trayIndex)
      Get all external feed streams connected to a tray.

      Feeds registered through addFeedStream(StreamInterface, int) are always included. The method also includes named streams added directly to the tray to preserve legacy workflows that use getTray(index).addStream(stream) for side feeds or stripping gas.

      Parameters:
      trayIndex - tray index to inspect
      Returns:
      external feed streams connected to the tray

    • recordConvergence

      private void recordConvergence(double[] entry)
      Append a residual snapshot to the convergence history, capping at MAX_CONVERGENCE_HISTORY entries.
      Parameters:
      entry - residual array to record

    • applyMurphreeCorrection

      private void applyMurphreeCorrection(int trayIndex)
      Apply Murphree tray efficiency correction to the vapor leaving a tray. The correction blends the equilibrium vapor composition with the inlet vapor composition: 
       y_i^out = y_i^in + E_MV * (y_i^eq - y_i^in)
      where E_MV is the Murphree efficiency, y_i^eq is the equilibrium composition from the flash, and y_i^in is the inlet vapor composition. When efficiency is 1.0, the tray is ideal and no correction is applied. The correction is skipped for reboilers and condensers (first and last trays).
      Parameters:
      trayIndex - index of the tray in the trays list

    • applyRelaxation

      private StreamInterface applyRelaxation(StreamInterface previous, StreamInterface current, double relaxation)
      Blend the current stream update with the previous iterate using the provided relaxation factor.
      Parameters:
      previous - stream from the previous iteration (may be null)
      current - current iteration stream
      relaxation - relaxation factor applied to the update
      Returns:
      relaxed stream instance to be used in the next tear

    • applyRelaxationFast

      private StreamInterface applyRelaxationFast(StreamInterface previous, StreamInterface current, double relaxation)
      Blend streams using the fast unchanged-clone path for fixed-point sweeps.
      Parameters:
      previous - stream from the previous iteration (may be null)
      current - current iteration stream
      relaxation - relaxation factor applied to the update
      Returns:
      relaxed stream instance to be used in the next tear

    • applyRelaxationInternal

      private StreamInterface applyRelaxationInternal(StreamInterface previous, StreamInterface current, double relaxation, boolean skipUnchangedReflash)
      Blend the current stream update with the previous iterate.
      Parameters:
      previous - stream from the previous iteration (may be null)
      current - current iteration stream
      relaxation - relaxation factor applied to the update
      skipUnchangedReflash - whether an unchanged clone can reuse the current stream flash state
      Returns:
      relaxed stream instance to be used in the next tear

      private boolean canRelaxMolarComposition(StreamInterface previous, StreamInterface current, StreamInterface relaxed, double[] previousComposition, double[] currentComposition)
      Check whether two stream compositions can be relaxed component-by-component.
      Parameters:
      previous - previous internal stream state
      current - current internal stream state
      relaxed - relaxed stream receiving the mixed composition
      previousComposition - previous molar composition vector
      currentComposition - current molar composition vector
      Returns:

      private double getRelaxedInternalFlow(double flow, double maximumInternalFlow)

      maximumInternalFlow - maximum emergency flow magnitude in kg/hr
      Returns:
