    • finalizeSolve

      private void finalizeSolve(UUID id, int iterations, double temperatureResidual, double massResidual, double energyResidual, long startTime)
      Finalise a successful solver run by updating iteration metrics and product streams.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier
      iterations - number of iterations performed
      temperatureResidual - final average temperature residual
      massResidual - final relative mass residual
      energyResidual - final relative energy residual
      startTime - nano time when the solve started

    • synchronizeColumnEndProductStreams

      private void synchronizeColumnEndProductStreams(UUID id)
      Synchronize condenser and reboiler product streams with the exposed column products.

      Product reconciliation updates gasOutStream and liquidOutStream. Legacy callers may also read the condenser product or reboiler liquid stream directly, so those equipment-level product streams must be kept on the same balanced basis.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier to assign to synchronized product streams

    • synchronizeProductStream

      private void synchronizeProductStream(StreamInterface target, StreamInterface source, UUID id)
      Copy a source product basis into a target product stream.
      Parameters:
      target - target product stream to synchronize
      source - source product stream containing the balanced thermodynamic system
      id - calculation identifier to assign to the target

    • synchronizeTerminalProductDrawStreams

      private void synchronizeTerminalProductDrawStreams(UUID id)
      Synchronize product-draw residual diagnostics with the final exposed column products.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier to assign to synchronized diagnostic streams

      private void updateLastSolveStatus(boolean productReconciled, boolean fallbackProductsApplied)
      Update the strict solve status after product handling and residual diagnostics are current.
      Parameters:
      productReconciled - true if public products were materially reconciled
      fallbackProductsApplied - true if fallback products were generated

      private void setLastSolveStatus(DistillationColumn.SolveStatus status, String reason)

      private void capInternalTrayTraffic()
      Cap cached internal tray outlet streams to the emergency traffic limit.

    • updateProductsFromExternalComponentBalance

      private boolean updateProductsFromExternalComponentBalance(UUID id)
      Updates products so the exposed streams close the external component balance.
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier to assign to the updated stream
      Returns:
      true if product component amounts were materially changed

    • componentMolesMateriallyDiffer

      private boolean componentMolesMateriallyDiffer(double[] before, double[] after)
      Check whether two component-flow vectors differ materially for solve-status classification.
      Parameters:
      before - component mole amounts before reconciliation
      after - component mole amounts after reconciliation
      Returns:
      true when relative component drift exceeds the status tolerance

    • captureTerminalProductDrawStreams

      private void captureTerminalProductDrawStreams(UUID id)
      Captures the raw terminal tray draw streams used by product-draw residual diagnostics.

      Product reconciliation and guarded fallback updates can change the public column products after the tray solver has produced terminal draws. MESH diagnostics must compare public products to these raw terminal draws rather than to synchronized clones of the public products.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier to assign to the captured draw streams

    • updateProductStreamFromComponentMoles

      private void updateProductStreamFromComponentMoles(StreamInterface productStream, double[] componentMoles, UUID id)
      Replaces a product stream fluid with the same thermodynamic model at the current stream temperature and pressure but with specified component mole amounts.
      Parameters:
      productStream - stream to update
      componentMoles - component mole amounts on the stream-flow basis
      id - calculation identifier to assign after the update

    • updateProductStreamWithForcedPhase

      private void updateProductStreamWithForcedPhase(StreamInterface productStream, double[] componentMoles, String phaseTypeName, UUID id)
      Replace a product stream fluid with the same thermodynamic model at the product stream's own temperature and pressure, forcing a single phase identity instead of running a TP flash.

      Used as a phase-preserving rescue when an accelerator solver (Inside-Out, Matrix-IO, Newton) converges to a tray-0 oil-phase composition that, when re-flashed in isolation at the reboiler T/P, collapses to single-phase gas. The tray system itself was two-phase by construction, so the moles drawn from it are valid; forcing the phase preserves the rigorous solver result and avoids the spurious overall-feed-flash fallback that otherwise triggers via bottomProductPhaseInvalid().

      Parameters:
      productStream - stream to update
      componentMoles - component mole amounts on the stream-flow basis
      phaseTypeName - phase type description ("gas" or "liquid")
      id - calculation identifier to assign after the update

    • updateProductsFromOverallFeedFlash

      private boolean updateProductsFromOverallFeedFlash(UUID id)
      Update public products from an overall equilibrium flash of all external feeds.

      This fallback is used only after the tray solver has produced non-physical internal traffic. It gives bounded, mass-conserving products for diagnostics without claiming that the rigorous tray MESH problem has converged.

      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier to assign to fallback products
      Returns:
      true if fallback products were created

    • updateProductsFromShortcutEquilibriumSplit

      private boolean updateProductsFromShortcutEquilibriumSplit(SystemInterface feedSystem, UUID id)
      Create fallback products from a bounded shortcut equilibrium split.

      The split uses bottom terminal K-values and a Rachford-Rice vapor-fraction estimate to place volatile components preferentially in the vapor and heavy components preferentially in the liquid. It is only used when an overall TP flash does not expose both gas and liquid phases.

      Parameters:
      feedSystem - combined external feed system
      id - calculation identifier to assign to fallback products
      Returns:
      true if shortcut fallback products were created

    • estimateShortcutVaporFraction

      private double estimateShortcutVaporFraction(double[] feedComponentMoles, SystemInterface kSystem, double fallbackVaporFraction)
      Estimate a bounded vapor fraction for shortcut fallback products from K-values.
      Parameters:
      feedComponentMoles - feed component mole amounts
      kSystem - initialized system carrying component K-values at fallback conditions
      fallbackVaporFraction - vapor fraction to use if the estimate cannot be calculated
      Returns:
      vapor fraction clamped away from zero and one

    • evaluateRachfordRiceResidual

      private double evaluateRachfordRiceResidual(double[] feedComponentMoles, SystemInterface kSystem, double totalMoles, double vaporFraction)
      Evaluate the Rachford-Rice residual for a trial vapor fraction.
      Parameters:
      feedComponentMoles - feed component mole amounts
      kSystem - initialized system carrying component K-values
      totalMoles - total positive feed moles
      vaporFraction - trial vapor fraction from zero to one
      Returns:
      Rachford-Rice residual

    • clampShortcutVaporFraction

      private double clampShortcutVaporFraction(double vaporFraction)
      Clamp shortcut vapor fractions to keep both fallback product streams populated.
      Parameters:
      vaporFraction - vapor fraction to clamp
      Returns:
      finite vapor fraction in the range 0.05 to 0.95

    • getCurrentProductVaporFraction

      private double getCurrentProductVaporFraction()
      Calculate the current public vapor product mole fraction.
      Returns:
      vapor fraction bounded away from zero and one

      productStream - stream to update
      componentMoles - component mole amounts on the stream-flow basis
      phaseTypeName - phase type name to assign to the product
      id - calculation identifier to assign to the product

      phaseTypeName - phase type name to locate

      phaseIndex - phase index to extract

      phaseIndex - phase index to normalize

      Returns:
      finite absolute flow, or positive infinity if the stream is non-finite

    • getFeedComponentMoles

      private double[] getFeedComponentMoles()
      Calculates total component mole amounts entering the column through all external feeds.
      Returns:
      component mole amounts on the stream-flow basis used by NeqSim streams, or an empty array if external feeds do not share a common component basis

    • getSideDrawComponentMoles

      private double[] getSideDrawComponentMoles(int componentCount)
      Calculates component mole amounts withdrawn in all side-draw streams.
      Parameters:
      componentCount - number of components expected in the feed/product basis
      Returns:
      component mole amounts withdrawn through side draws

    • getNumberOfComponentsFromFeeds

      private int getNumberOfComponentsFromFeeds()
      Gets the number of components from the first available feed stream.
      Returns:
      number of components, or zero when no feeds are connected

    • getComponentMoles

      private double[] getComponentMoles(SystemInterface system)
      Calculates component mole amounts from all phases in a thermodynamic system.
      Parameters:
      system - thermodynamic system to inspect
      Returns:
      component mole amounts on the system-flow basis

    • getPhaseFilteredComponentMoles

      private double[] getPhaseFilteredComponentMoles(SystemInterface system, boolean gasPhase)
      Sum component mole amounts contributed by gas-like or liquid-like phases only.

      Tray terminal thermo systems can hold both an ascending gas phase and a descending liquid phase. When reconciling a single-phase public product against the external feed mass balance, the moles attributed to the product must come only from the relevant phase. If no matching phase is present (e.g. a single-phase oil reboiler or pure-vapor distillate), this method falls back to getComponentMoles(SystemInterface) so the reconciliation step still has a non-zero composition to scale.

      Parameters:
      system - thermodynamic system to inspect
      gasPhase - true to sum gas-like phases, false to sum oil/liquid/aqueous phases
      Returns:
      component mole amounts contributed by the matching phases

    • resetLastSolveMetrics

      private void resetLastSolveMetrics()
      Reset cached solve metrics when no calculation is performed.

    • resetInsideOutTelemetry

      private void resetInsideOutTelemetry()
      Reset rigorous inside-out telemetry fields.

    • resetNaphtaliTelemetry

      private void resetNaphtaliTelemetry()
      Reset Naphtali-Sandholm telemetry fields.

    • resetMatrixInsideOutDiagnostics

      private void resetMatrixInsideOutDiagnostics()
      Reset matrix inside-out warm-start diagnostics.

    • energyBalanceCheck

      public void energyBalanceCheck()
      Prints a simple energy balance for each tray to the console. The method calculates the total enthalpy of all inlet streams and compares it with the outlet enthalpy in order to highlight any discrepancies in the column setup.

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      The main method demonstrates the creation and operation of a distillation column using the NeqSim library. It performs the following steps:
      1. Creates a test thermodynamic system with methane, ethane, and propane components.
      2. Performs a TP flash calculation on the test system.
      3. Creates two separate feed streams from the test system.
      4. Constructs a distillation column with 5 trays, a reboiler, and a condenser.
      5. Adds the two feed streams to the distillation column at tray 3.
      6. Builds and runs the process system.
      7. Displays the results of the distillation column, including the gas and liquid output streams.
      Parameters:
      args - command line arguments (not used)

    • validateSetup

      public ValidationResult validateSetup()
      Validate the process equipment before execution.

      Checks for common setup errors:

      • Equipment has a valid name
      • Input streams connected
      • Operating parameters in valid ranges
      Specified by:
      validateSetup in interface ProcessEquipmentInterface
      Returns:
      validation result with errors and warnings

    • validateSpecifications

      public ValidationResult validateSpecifications()
      Validate only the configured top and bottom column specifications.

      This focused validator is useful before a solve when users are scripting product-purity, component-recovery, flow-rate, reflux, or duty specifications and want actionable diagnostics without validating the full equipment setup.

      Returns:
      validation result containing specification errors and warnings

    • validateCommercialActiveBounds

      private void validateCommercialActiveBounds(ValidationResult result)
      Add warnings for valid but numerically severe active-bound specifications.
      Parameters:
      result - validation result receiving active-bound warnings

    • validateSpecificationActiveBound

      private void validateSpecificationActiveBound(ValidationResult result, ColumnSpecification specification)

      Parameters:
      result - validation result receiving active-bound warnings
      specification - column specification to screen

      Returns:
      true when the value lies close to either hard bound

    • getNumerOfTrays

      public int getNumerOfTrays()

      getNumerOfTrays.

      Returns:
      a int

    • getNumberOfTrays

      public int getNumberOfTrays()
      Get the number of stages in the column using the correctly spelled API name.

      This method is an alias for the legacy getNumerOfTrays() method and is kept separate to preserve backwards compatibility with existing scripts.

      Returns:
      number of stages in the column including reboiler and condenser stages when present

    • setSeedTemperature

      public void setSeedTemperature(int stageIndex, double temperatureK)
      Set a per-stage seed temperature used as an initial guess by residual solvers.

      A seed temperature is not a tray specification. Unlike Mixer.setOutTemperature(double), it does not pin the stage temperature or replace the energy balance. The current DistillationColumn.SolverType.NAPHTALI_SANDHOLM implementation uses finite seeds only when the same stage has no fixed output-temperature specification.

      Parameters:
      stageIndex - bottom-up stage index, where zero is the reboiler when present and numberOfTrays - 1 is the top stage
      temperatureK - seed temperature in kelvin; pass Double.NaN to clear a stage seed

    • getSeedTemperature

      public double getSeedTemperature(int stageIndex)
      Get the seed temperature configured for one stage.
      Parameters:
      stageIndex - bottom-up stage index to inspect
      Returns:
      seed temperature in kelvin, or Double.NaN when no seed is configured or the stage index is outside the current column range

    • hasSeedTemperatures

      public boolean hasSeedTemperatures()
      Check whether at least one finite stage seed temperature is configured.
      Returns:
      true when one or more stages have a finite seed temperature

    • clearSeedTemperatures

      public void clearSeedTemperatures()
      Clear all per-stage seed temperatures.

    • ensureSeedTemperatureArray

      private void ensureSeedTemperatureArray()
      Ensure the seed-temperature array exists and matches the current stage count.

    • setMurphreeEfficiency

      public void setMurphreeEfficiency(double efficiency)
      Set the Murphree tray efficiency for all stages without explicit overrides.
      Parameters:
      efficiency - value between 0.0 (no separation) and 1.0 (ideal equilibrium stage)

    • setMurphreeEfficiency

      public void setMurphreeEfficiency(int stage, double efficiency)
      Set the Murphree tray efficiency for a single stage.

      Stage numbering follows the column internal order: stage 0 is the reboiler when present and the last stage is the condenser when present. Reboiler and condenser stages are still treated as equilibrium stages by the correction algorithm, but the value is stored so external style scripts can round-trip stage efficiency data consistently.

      Parameters:
      stage - 0-based stage index in the range [0, numberOfTrays)
      efficiency - value between 0.0 (no separation) and 1.0 (ideal equilibrium stage)

    • setMurphreeEfficiencies

      public void setMurphreeEfficiencies(double[] efficiencies)
      Set Murphree tray efficiencies for every stage in one call.

      The array length must equal the total number of stages including reboiler and condenser if they are present. Entries equal to Double.NaN restore use of the column-wide default for that stage.

      Parameters:
      efficiencies - per-stage Murphree efficiencies, or null to clear all overrides

    • clearPerStageMurphreeEfficiency

      public void clearPerStageMurphreeEfficiency()
      Clear all per-stage Murphree efficiency overrides.

    • getMurphreeEfficiency

      public double getMurphreeEfficiency()
      Retrieve the current column-wide Murphree tray efficiency.
      Returns:
      Murphree efficiency used as the default for stages without overrides

    • getMurphreeEfficiency

      public double getMurphreeEfficiency(int stage)
      Retrieve the effective Murphree tray efficiency for a single stage.
      Parameters:
      stage - 0-based stage index in the range [0, numberOfTrays)
      Returns:
      per-stage override when set, otherwise the column-wide Murphree efficiency

    • ensurePerStageMurphreeEfficiencyArray

      private void ensurePerStageMurphreeEfficiencyArray()
      Ensure that the per-stage Murphree efficiency array exists and matches the current stage count.

    • getEffectiveMurphreeEfficiency

      private double getEffectiveMurphreeEfficiency(int stage)
      Get the effective Murphree efficiency for a stage without performing bounds validation.
      Parameters:
      stage - 0-based stage index
      Returns:
      per-stage override when finite, otherwise the column-wide Murphree efficiency

    • clampMurphreeEfficiency

      private double clampMurphreeEfficiency(double efficiency)
      Clamp a Murphree efficiency into the supported interval.
      Parameters:
      efficiency - requested efficiency value
      Returns:
      value clamped to the interval [0.0, 1.0]

    • setInnerLoopSteps

      public void setInnerLoopSteps(int steps)
      Set the number of simplified inner-loop iterations between rigorous flash updates in the IO solver. A value of 0 disables the simplified model (all iterations use rigorous flash).
      Parameters:
      steps - number of inner-loop steps (0 to disable, typically 2-5)

    • getInnerLoopSteps

      public int getInnerLoopSteps()
      Return the current number of simplified inner-loop iterations per outer flash update.
      Returns:
      inner-loop step count

    • getConvergenceHistory

      public List<double[]> getConvergenceHistory()
      Return the per-iteration convergence history from the most recent solve.

      Each entry is a three-element array: [temperatureResidual, massResidual, energyResidual].

      Returns:
      list of residual arrays, one per iteration; empty if no solve has been run

    • getSolverType

      public DistillationColumn.SolverType getSolverType()
      Retrieve the currently selected solver type.
      Returns:
      solver type enum value

    • getLastSolverTypeUsed

      public DistillationColumn.SolverType getLastSolverTypeUsed()
      Get the solver strategy that completed the latest run.

      For explicitly selected solvers this is normally the same as getSolverType(). When DistillationColumn.SolverType.AUTO is configured, this reports the concrete solver selected by the automatic solver factory.

      Returns:
      solver strategy used by the latest solve

    • getLastSolveStatus

      public DistillationColumn.SolveStatus getLastSolveStatus()
      Get the strict status of the latest column solve.
      Returns:
      latest solve status

    • getLastSolveStatusReason

      public String getLastSolveStatusReason()
      Get the explanatory reason for the latest solve status.
      Returns:
      concise status reason, or an empty string if none is available

    • getLastAutoSolverSummary

      public String getLastAutoSolverSummary()
      Get the latest automatic solver candidate trace.
      Returns:
      candidate trace from DistillationColumn.SolverType.AUTO, or an empty string when automatic mode was not used in the latest solve

    • getLastAutoFeasibilityReport

      public String getLastAutoFeasibilityReport()

      Returns:
      feasibility report text, or an empty string when AUTO has not screened the column

    • getLastInitializationReport

      public String getLastInitializationReport()

      Returns:
      initialization report text, or an empty string when no seed was attempted

    • getLastAutoSolverHistory

      unmodifiable automatic solver event list

      list of SimpleTray objects

      Returns:
      array of liquid holdups indexed by tray number, or null

    • getTrayEnthalpy

      public double[] getTrayEnthalpy()
      Returns the per-tray enthalpy array in J. May be null if energy balance has not been initialized.
      Returns:
      array of tray enthalpies indexed by tray number, or null

    • setTrayDryPressureDrop

      public void setTrayDryPressureDrop(double dpPa)
      Sets the dry tray pressure drop per tray in Pa. Used in the dynamic vapor hydraulic model to compute vapor flow rate as a function of pressure difference between trays.
      Parameters:
      dpPa - dry tray pressure drop in Pascals (positive value)

    • getTrayDryPressureDrop

      public double getTrayDryPressureDrop()
      Returns the dry tray pressure drop per tray in Pa.
      Returns:
      dry tray pressure drop in Pa

    • setDynamicEnergyEnabled

      public void setDynamicEnergyEnabled(boolean enabled)
      Enables or disables the per-tray energy balance in dynamic mode. When enabled, each tray's enthalpy is tracked and PH flash is used for re-equilibration instead of TP flash.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to enable energy-balanced trays

    • isDynamicEnergyEnabled

      public boolean isDynamicEnergyEnabled()
      Returns whether the per-tray energy balance is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if energy-balanced trays are active

    • runTransient

      public void runTransient(double dt, UUID id)

      runTransient

      This method calculates thermodynamic and unit operations using difference equations if available and calculateSteadyState is true. Use setCalculateSteadyState to set the parameter. Sets calc identifier UUID.

      Dynamic distillation column model. When dynamicColumnEnabled is true, performs a single forward-Euler integration step on each tray's liquid holdup using the MESH equations. Liquid leaving each tray is calculated using the Francis weir overflow formula.

      Specified by:
      runTransient in interface SimulationInterface
      Parameters:
      dt - time step in seconds
      id - calculation identifier
